dictionaryVocabulary.description: A polite opener for getting attention, apologizing, or starting a request.

example.japanese: すみません、ちょっと手伝ってください。
example.kana: すみません、ちょっとてつだってください。
example.english: Excuse me, please help me for a moment.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used when helping someone with a task.

example.japanese: 宿題を手伝ってください。
example.kana: しゅくだいをてつだってください。
example.english: Please help me with my homework.

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used to ask if someone is okay or to say that something is fine.
Used for seeing, watching, or intentionally looking at something.

example.japanese: この切符を見てください。
example.kana: このきっぷをみてください。

dictionaryVocabulary.description: Used when helping someone in trouble or rescuing them.

misconceptions.error: Starts a request with casual ねえ in a public or service context.
misconceptions.mitigation: Comparison card 1 contrasts public attention-getters; response-selection drills use formal request openers.

misconceptions.error: Uses bare imperative forms when asking for help.
misconceptions.mitigation: Formula-fill drills practice てください request frames.

misconceptions.error: Answers 大丈夫ですか as if it asks for identity or location.
misconceptions.mitigation: Multiple-choice and response-selection drills treat it as a welfare/help check.

misconceptions.error: Uses casual 手伝って with a stranger or staff member.
misconceptions.mitigation: Comparison cards and response-selection distractors mark wrong-register choices.

misconceptions.error: Translates ちょっと literally as only 'a little' and misses its request-softening role.
misconceptions.mitigation: Dialogue scenes and reading-practice cards show ちょっとすみません as a help-request opener.

exercises.title: Asking for Help - Formal · Casual · Slang
exercises.intro: Help requests depend on relationship. Start with formal public phrases, then learn casual friend-to-friend forms and compressed slang you should understand before using.
rows: すみません、手伝ってください。
Please help me - stranger, staff, teacher
Excuse me / could I ask something?
Are you okay? / Do you need help?
もう一度お願いします。
ゆっくり話してください。
はい、ゆっくり話します。
exercises.takeaway: Use すみません + てください with strangers. Use shorter forms only with people close to you.

lines.speaker: キム
lines.jp: すみません、ちょっと手伝ってください。
lines.en: Excuse me, please help me for a moment.

lines.speaker: キム
lines.jp: この切符を見てください。大丈夫ですか。
lines.romaji: Kono kippu wo mite kudasai. Daijoubu desu ka.
lines.en: Please look at this ticket. Is it okay?

lines.speaker: みか
lines.jp: ねえ、ちょっといい？ 宿題を手伝って。
lines.romaji: Nee, chotto ii? Shukudai wo tetsudatte.
lines.en: Hey, do you have a second? Help me with homework.

lines.speaker: そうた
lines.jp: いいよ。どこがわからない？
lines.en: Sure. What part don't you understand?

lines.speaker: みか
lines.en: Here. Say it one more time.

lines.speaker: はる
lines.jp: やばい、マジでむり。助けて！
lines.en: This is bad, I seriously can't do it. Help!

lines.speaker: ゆー
lines.jp: どうした？ ちょい待って。

exercises.prompt: Which opener fits asking station staff for help?
exercises.title: Getting Attention Politely

variants.label: FORMAL
variants.jp: すみません、ちょっと手伝ってください。
variants.en: Excuse me, please help me for a moment.

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.wrongExplanation: This is too casual for staff or strangers.

variants.label: SLANG
variants.wrongExplanation: This sounds emotional and intimate, not like a public request.

exercises.takeaway: すみません opens public requests safely.
exercises.speakText: すみません、ちょっと手伝ってください。

exercises.prompt: Which phrase asks someone to speak slowly?
exercises.title: Requesting Slower Speech

variants.label: CORRECT
variants.jp: ゆっくり話してください。

variants.label: COMMON MIXUP
variants.jp: もう一度お願いします。
variants.note: This asks for repetition, not slower speed.
variants.wrongExplanation: This asks for repetition, not speed.

exercises.takeaway: もう一度 = repeat. ゆっくり = slowly.
exercises.speakText: ゆっくり話してください。

exercises.prompt: Which check asks 'Are you okay?' politely?
exercises.title: Checking on Someone

variants.label: CASUAL
variants.wrongExplanation: This is fine with friends but too bare for a polite check.

variants.label: WRONG MEANING
variants.note: Asks what happened after you have attention.
variants.wrongExplanation: This asks what happened, not whether someone is okay.

exercises.takeaway: 大丈夫ですか is the safe polite check.

exercises.prompt: Which answer naturally accepts a help request?
exercises.title: Accepting Help Requests

variants.label: TOO CASUAL
variants.wrongExplanation: This is too casual for a polite service-style exchange.

variants.label: MISMATCH
variants.note: A greeting from lesson 01, not a help response.
variants.wrongExplanation: This is a prior greeting, not a response to a request.

exercises.takeaway: いいですよ accepts a request without sounding too casual.

exercises.prompt: Complete the polite help request:
exercises.speakText: すみません、手伝ってください。

exercises.prompt: Ask someone to speak slowly:
exercises.speakText: ゆっくり話してください。

exercises.prompt: Ask for one more repetition:

slot.hint: Set phrase meaning one more time.

exercises.speakText: もう一度お願いします。

cards.back: Chotto sumimasen - Excuse me for a moment
Daijoubu desu ka - Are you okay?

cards.front: もう一度お願いします
cards.back: Mou ichido onegaishimasu - One more time, please
cards.speakText: もう一度お願いします

cards.front: ゆっくり話してください
cards.back: Yukkuri hanashite kudasai - Please speak slowly
cards.speakText: ゆっくり話してください

cards.back: Ii desu yo - That's fine / sure

exercises.prompt: Match each expression to its meaning:

pairs.left: もう一度お願いします
ゆっくり話してください

cards.jp: すみません、ちょっと手伝ってください。
cards.en: Excuse me, please help me for a moment.

comprehension.q: Is this safe for a stranger?
comprehension.a: Yes. すみません plus ください is polite and beginner-safe.

cards.jp: この切符を見てください。大丈夫ですか。
cards.romaji: Kono kippu wo mite kudasai. Daijoubu desu ka.
cards.en: Please look at this ticket. Is it okay?

comprehension.a: The ticket. The speaker asks whether it is okay.

cards.jp: ねえ、ちょっといい？ 宿題を手伝って。
cards.romaji: Nee, chotto ii? Shukudai wo tetsudatte.
cards.en: Hey, do you have a second? Help me with homework.

cards.jp: いいよ。どこがわからない？
cards.en: Sure. What part don't you understand?
Here. Say it one more time.

cards.jp: やばい、マジでむり。助けて！
cards.en: This is bad, I seriously can't do it. Help!

comprehension.q: Should a beginner use this with staff?
comprehension.a: No. It is emotional slang for close relationships or urgent situations.

cards.jp: どうした？ ちょい待って。
ゆっくり話してください。もう一度お願いします。
cards.romaji: Yukkuri hanashite kudasai. Mou ichido onegaishimasu.
cards.en: Please speak slowly. One more time, please.
